channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Readers Share Quirky Observations and Questions in Column 8
item.description: This Column 8 edition of The Sydney Morning Herald features a light-hearted mix of reader observations, questions, and personal anecdotes. Gary Bryson from Blackheath questions the tone of roadside sp...

item.title: Suspect dies after falling from height while fleeing from police in Taguig
item.description: A man lost his life in Taguig City after jumping from a considerable height while trying to escape police pursuit. According to initial information from authorities, the suspect was being chased by po...

item.title: Mercedes-Benz launches A200 Night Edition in South Africa with premium styling and tech upgrades
item.description: Mercedes-Benz has introduced the A200 Night Edition to the South African market, adding a stylish variant to its compact luxury car lineup. The four-door hatchback distinguishes itself with exclusive ...

item.title: Philippine Agriculture Faces P75 Billion Loss Due to Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) is warning of potentially devastating losses amounting to P75 billion if the government doesn’t provide assistance to rice and corn farmers, as well as fisherfolk. T...

item.title: Andrew Johns calls for change to sin bin penalty time in NRL
item.description: NRL legend Andrew Johns has called for a change to the amount of time players are required to spend in the sin bin for professional fouls. Currently, players can be sent to the sin bin for 10 minutes ...

item.title: Policyholders seek compensation over failure of three insurance companies
item.description: A group of frustrated insurance customers has moved to court to sue the Insurance Regulatory Authority (IRA) following the collapse of three insurers—Resolution Insurance, Standard Assurance, and Conc...

item.title: Analysis of the Canadian Bar Association's response to criticism of judicial rulings
item.description: The Canadian Bar Association (CBA) recently issued a news release warning the media about the potential dangers of delegitimizing judges, following critical commentary on Justice Faisal Mirza of the O...

item.title: Fed March Meeting Minutes May Reveal Economic Risks from U.S.-Iran Conflict
item.description: The U.S. Federal Reserve held its March 17-18 meeting amid a developing global oil shock caused by the U.S.-Iran conflict, which had pushed oil prices from around US$70 to US$100 a barrel. Federal Res...

item.title: Mustafa Kizza's Impressive Goals Reflect His Ambition in Kenyan Football
item.description: Mustafa Kizza, a left back for Nairobi United, has quickly made his mark in the Kenyan Premier League with two stunning goals. The Ugandan player scored his first goal for the club in February, direct...

item.title: Rangers defender Dujon Sterling faces legal proceedings over alleged drink-driving offence
item.description: The article reports that Rangers footballer Dujon Sterling has appeared in court to face a charge related to drink-driving. According to available details, Sterling, a professional defender for Ranger...

item.title: Bukoba Municipality Sees Key Development Projects Progressing on Schedule
item.description: Bukoba Urban MP, Engineer Johnstone Mutasingwa, has reassured the local community that major development projects in the municipality are progressing well and are expected to be completed on time. The...

item.title: President Marcos assures public of good health amid rumors
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. reassured Filipinos on Wednesday that he is in good health, urging the public not to believe social media rumors regarding his condition. During a brief excha...

item.title: Enugu suspends daily levies for petty traders to support small businesses
item.description: The Enugu State Government don announce say dem don stop the daily tolls wey petty traders dey pay for markets across the State. Dis move na to protect people wey no get much money and to ginger Micro...

item.title: TMC alleges Election Commissioner dismissed concerns on Bengal polls, asked them to leave
item.description: Trinamool Congress (TMC) leaders have claimed that the Chief Election Commissioner, Gyanesh Kumar, told a TMC delegation to ‘get lost’ during a meeting at the Election Commission’s Delhi office. The d...

item.title: Jet Fuel Supply Recovery Expected to Take Months After Hormuz Reopening
item.description: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) don raise alarm say e go take plenty months before jet fuel supply return to normal, even after Iran don open di Strait of Hormuz again. Di crisis st...

item.title: Ramaphosa stresses non-interference and quiet diplomacy to newly accredited diplomats
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa formally accredited the new US ambassador, Leo Brent Bozell III, along with ambassadors from 19 other countries, at the Sefako Makgatho Presidential Guesthouse in Pretoria. D...

item.title: Government details steps to enhance education quality through curriculum reform and teacher recruitment
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced a comprehensive plan to put into action the 2014 Education and Training Policy by focusing on several key areas aimed at improving the country’s education syst...

item.title: Gachagua Urges Respect for Ruto at Kiaraho Memorial
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua publicly urged supporters to refrain from heckling President William Ruto during a memorial service for the late Ol Kalou MP, David Kiaraho. The service, held ...

item.title: Puducherry Assembly Elections 2026: Constituencies and Poll Preparations
item.description: Polling preparations are in full swing in Puducherry ahead of the Assembly elections scheduled for 9th April 2026. The Election Commission has enhanced security and contingency measures, including pro...

item.title: Indian Markets See Recovery After US-Iran Tensions Ease
item.description: The Indian stock markets, which had taken a bit of a hammering due to the heightened tensions between the United States and Iran, have managed to recover a significant chunk of the losses – around 65%...
